title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg affirmed his commitment to the Mavericks, stating he loves playing there and doesn't want to go elsewhere.
Flagg, a Maine native, made his first NBA appearance at TD Garden against the Celtics, the team he grew up supporting.
Selected as the No. 1 overall pick, Flagg averages 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds, and is under contract with Dallas until at least 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics after 42 weeks, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists.
Tatum struggled initially, missing his first six shots, but found his rhythm later, aiding in a 25-point win over the Mavericks.
The Celtics sit second in the Eastern Conference; Tatum's return aims to boost playoff readiness with several weeks left in the season.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' lineup after nearly 10 months without a minutes restriction and contributed 15 points.
Neemias Queta impressed with 16 points and 15 rebounds, benefiting from Tatum's return to the game.
Nikola Vucevic will be out for a month after suffering a fractured ring finger; he is set for surgery.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins did not make major trades at the deadline, focusing on long-term rebuilding plans.
General Manager Don Sweeney stated the team explored options but avoided high-cost, short-term deals.
Bruins traded minor league assets and retained four first-round picks over the next two years.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby will be traded to the Baltimore Ravens from the Las Vegas Raiders for two first-round picks.
Crosby reportedly desired to play for Patriots' coach Mike Vrabel, but New England was outbid by Baltimore.
Patriots retain over $60 million cap space for possible trades or signings, despite missing out on Crosby.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic will miss approximately a month due to a fractured right ring finger.
Vucevic sustained the injury during the first half against the Mavericks, playing only two minutes.
Vucevic has been averaging 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds for the Celtics since the trade deadline.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to TD Garden for his first game after recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum received a warm welcome from fans before the Celtics' game against the Dallas Mavericks on Causeway Street.
Tatum played six minutes in his return, scoring a put-back dunk and a step-back 3-pointer in the second quarter.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, per ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade frees up $5.7 million in cap space, boosting Patriots' potential free agency spending to over $60 million.
Bears require a new center after Drew Dalman's retirement, while Patriots may promote Jared Wilson to replace Bradbury.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The film 'Hamnet' explores Shakespeare's personal life, streaming on Peacock.
'The Secret Agent' set in Brazil is available on Hulu.
'Arrested Development' will be removed from Netflix on March 15.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics starting lineup against the Dallas Mavericks after almost ten months of recovery.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication during his rehabilitation process.
Tatum replaced Baylor Scheierman in the lineup; no mention of minutes restriction by Coach Mazzulla.
